assumptions: The developer has a supported browser.
No Snyk account, Organization, product, SCM integration, or CLI scan target has been selected yet.

developer_goal: Create a Snyk account and reach the first documented security scan result for a chosen product and integration interface.

first_success.outcome: The selected Snyk product and interface produce a first issue report for a Project or local target.
first_success.observable_signal: Depends on the unresolved route; for CLI Open Source, the terminal displays the issue report and remediation guidance after snyk test.

route_selection.surface: Snyk Web UI getting started and Snyk CLI
route_selection.rule: Select the shortest complete route only after product and integration interface are fixed.

candidates.name: Web UI SCM integration and Project import
candidates.reason: Getting started makes repository integration conditional on the systems in use and treats SCM auth as a primary path.

candidates.name: CLI Open Source snyk test
candidates.reason: Docs present CLI as an alternative that avoids repository authentication, with its own install and auth steps.

candidates.name: CLI Code, Container, or IaC scan
candidates.reason: Start scanning documents peer commands with different prerequisites and result formats.

required_path.action: Create a free Snyk account or sign in at snyk.io.

required_fields.label: Snyk signup or SSO identity fields
required_fields.notes: Docs point to snyk.io and SSO or invitation flows but do not enumerate current required form fields.

required_path.observable_result: The developer is signed into the Snyk Web UI against an Organization.

required_path.action: Choose an integration path: authenticate an SCM integration or select CLI.

required_fields.label: Repository access if SCM is selected

required_path.observable_result: Snyk is connected to a scan source or the developer proceeds with CLI authentication.

required_path.action: Import a Project or run the selected CLI test command against a supported target.

required_fields.label: CLI auth token if using CLI

required_path.observable_result: Snyk displays or prints the first issue report for the selected product.

prerequisites.description: Supported languages, package managers, or frameworks are required for meaningful scans.
CLI Open Source scans often require the Project to be built first.

external_gates.description: Company SSO or Organization invitation may block a fresh personal signup from reaching the intended Organization.
SCM integrations require granting repository access outside Snyk.

platform_outcomes.description: Importing a Project triggers a scan and schedules recurring tests.
CLI test commands print vulnerability findings and remediation guidance for supported manifests.

excluded.item: Optional getting-started walkthrough prompts after account creation
excluded.reason: Official docs label the guided process optional.
Essentials is documented as Enterprise-plan only and is outside free first-scan success.

excluded.item: Fixing issues after the first report
excluded.reason: Review and fix actions occur after the first scan result.

sources.title: Getting started with the Snyk CLI
Snyk app login or sign up
evidence_supported: Public login or sign-up entry exists; required fields not fully enumerable without completing auth

uncertainties.question: Which Snyk product and interface define the first-mile scan: Web UI SCM import versus CLI Open Source, Code, Container, or IaC?
uncertainties.impact: The choice changes prerequisites, external permissions, commands, and the observable report format.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Choose one bounded route such as free-account CLI snyk test on a Node package.json, then verify signup through the terminal report.

uncertainties.question: What exact fields and identity-provider options appear on current Snyk signup?
uncertainties.impact: Signup field counts and possible email or SSO waits remain unverified.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated fresh-account capture of app.snyk.io registration without relying on company SSO.
